event_summary: As a fragile US-Iran ceasefire persisted, Trump said he would accept a 20-year suspension of Iran's nuclear program; US military planners simultaneously prepared an escalation option targeting nuclear and oil assets if diplomacy collapsed.
tldr: With only four headlines across four buckets, the corpus is small but the divergence is meaningful. Three headlines (Global Times, Haaretz, The New Arab) all frame the story through POLICY_PRESCRIPTION — deal terms, diplomatic meetings, and a US-China consensus that Iran cannot have nuclear weapons. Ukraine's Kyiv Post breaks toward SECURITY_DEFENSE with a headline about a US escalation plan targeting nuclear sites, the only title that treats the negotiations as primarily a military-option story. The China/Iran diplomatic meeting headline is the only one emphasising Tehran's agency in seeking international support rather than framing Iran as the object of US or Israeli demands.

evidence.quote: accept Iran suspending its nuclear program for 20 years
Trump, China's Xi 'agree Iran cannot have nuclear weapons'
Chinese, Iranian diplomats meet before US-Iran nuclear talk
US Prepares 'Escalation Plan' Targeting Iran's Nuclear
Trump, China's Xi 'agree Iran cannot have nuclear weapons'
Chinese, Iranian diplomats meet before US-Iran nuclear talk

isolation_top.note: Escalation-plan headline focuses on military option rather than diplomacy; diverges in semantic space from the three deal-terms titles.
Global Times headline uniquely frames China and Iran as diplomatic co-actors seeking international support, not as objects of US demands.
Haaretz headline is centred on Trump's offer and Iranian military capacity claims — a specific US domestic-politics angle.
Most central bucket: headline captures the US-China summit consensus, the most shared reference point across outlets.

bottom_line: The four-bucket headline corpus splits cleanly: three titles advertise diplomacy and deal terms, while Kyiv Post's headline alone flags the military escalation track — a framing divergence that reflects Ukraine's distinctive interest in preventing a US-Iran deal that might shift US attention and resources away from its own war.
